rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KINUKO ARIIZUMI JORGENSEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the administrator requested just 60 days more in his petition signed on 4/14/24, which time has already lapsed, and the petition did not make all of the allegations required per LRSF 14.35 J. A petition for final distribution (or to terminate the estate for lack of assets) or a second report of status must be filed at least two weeks before 9/9/24. Appearance will be required on 9/9/24 if no petition is timely filed.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PILPEL FAMILY TRUST DATED SEPTEMBER 25, 2006
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: --Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues to be cleared: (1) Notice of Hearing on Petition to Determine Claim to Property [DE-115] must be used. Also, the notice must identify all the assets subject to the petition. Prob. Code 851(c)(1). --New Notice of Hearing [DE-115] form was filed on 6/20/24; however, it was mailed on 6/20/24, failing to meet the 30-day service. (2) Notice of Directions for Remote Appearance must be given to all persons entitled to. LRSF. 14.1.C.6). (3) Petition to identify the specific assets of tangible personal property. --Petition has not been amended/supplemented. Petitioner merely submitted a revised proposed order. (4) Petition to provide further information regarding the mutual funds and LP assets subject to this petition regarding the current vesting, the date acquired etc. (5) Concerning verifications/signatures - (5-1) Petitioner verified the petition first (2/18/24), and the attorney signed (3/11/24) the petition. (5-2) Reply to Opposition was verified by petitioner (who is allegedly a fiduciary = trustee), but the signature looks different. Need explanation who verified and the proper authority to be presented unless the petitioner verified. (5-3) Clarification if Sylvia Pilpel's signature was signed by the declarant. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ZETTIE BERNETT HIGHTOWER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/29/24 to be heard with competing petition. Other issues that must be addressed before this petition can be heard: (1) The attorney's "supplemental declaration" e-filed on 6/13/24 AMENDED the petition. An amended petition should have been filed. At the very least, notice of the "supplemental declaration" must also be served and published. (2) Notice must have attached to it the CURRENT procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/19/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (This Court has not required the local form Declaration of Real Property since November 2020.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LINDA ANN CURRIE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/14/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of publication has been filed. (2) An ex-spouse has no standing. [Probate Code Section 8461.] Is he a Court-appointed guardian of the estate of the minor child? If so, please either cite the San Francisco case number or file a certified copy of Letters issued to the guardian. (3) Item 5a(3) or (4) of the petition must be answered. (4) Notice to a minor must be sent directly to the minor. A separate copy of the notice must be sent to the person or persons having legal custody of the minor, with whom the minor resides. [CRC 7.51(d).] Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/31/24. If the petitioner does not have legal standing and needs more time to open a guardianship of the estate for the minor, Counsel should contact the assigned examiner to request a further continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
